subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 03
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering famous landmarks, animals, science, healthy habits, and community for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these is a famous landmark located in India?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is a beautiful white marble mausoleum located in Agra, India.
questions.hint: It was built by an emperor for his wife.

questions.q: What is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah can run at speeds up to 70 miles per hour.
questions.hint: It has black spots and is very slender.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their traits.

questions.q: What is the main source of energy for all life on Earth?
questions.explanation: The sun provides the light and heat energy necessary for plants and animals to survive.
questions.hint: It appears in the sky every morning.
questions.objective: Understand basic scientific facts about energy.

questions.q: Which of the following is a healthy habit?
questions.explanation: Eating vegetables provides essential vitamins and minerals for a healthy body.
questions.hint: It is something you should have on your dinner plate.

questions.q: Who is the person that helps put out fires in our community?
questions.explanation: Firefighters are trained to fight fires and help keep the community safe.

questions.q: What planet is known as the 'Blue Planet'?
questions.explanation: Earth is called the Blue Planet because its surface is mostly covered by water.
questions.hint: It is the planet we live on.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which instrument belongs to the percussion family?
questions.explanation: Percussion instruments are played by being struck or scraped.
questions.hint: You hit these to make a beat.

questions.q: What is the freezing point of water in Celsius?
questions.explanation: Water turns into ice when it reaches 0 degrees Celsius.
questions.hint: It is the starting number for temperature.

questions.q: Which of these is a non-renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Coal is a fossil fuel that takes millions of years to form and cannot be easily replaced.
questions.hint: It is a black rock used to produce energy.

questions.q: Which country is home to the Kangaroo?
questions.explanation: Australia is famous for unique animals like the kangaroo and koala.
questions.hint: It is both a country and a continent.
